comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Code Simplifier**: The `affected_workflows` list is duplicated in docstring (lines 219-226) and created at runtime. Extract as a module-level constant to avoid duplication and runtime allocation - use it for both docstring and iteration.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Fixed — extracted `CLAUDE_CI_WORKFLOWS` as a module-level tuple constant. The Click docstring still contains the list inline since Click   literal blocks cannot reference Python constants, but the runtime loop now uses the constant.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-23 04:50 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Python Review**: Duplicate function definition - The `claude_ci()` function is defined multiple times in this file. The module-level constant `CLAUDE_CI_WORKFLOWS` has been extracted correctly, but the patch adds a third identical function definition at line 481 instead of removing the pre-existing duplicates. This will cause only the last definition to be used at runtime. Please remove the duplicate function definitions and keep only one implementation.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Fixed — removed the two duplicate claude_ci() definitions, keeping only the one using the CLAUDE_CI_WORKFLOWS constant. 174 lines deleted.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-23 05:02 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Code Simplifier**: This dict construction with conditional field addition can be simplified using direct assignment instead of declarative type annotation. Consider: `dedup_response = {...}; if existing_branch: dedup_response['branch_name'] = existing_branch` or use dict unpacking if needed - avoid premature type declaration before all fields are determined.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
Fixed — removed the premature type annotation. The dict is now constructed without an explicit type hint.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-23 04:50 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Code Simplifier**: The `copy_close_plan`, `copy_fix_conflicts_remote`, and `copy_address_remote` blocks all follow an identical pattern. Extract this as a helper to reduce duplication - the pattern is: format command, optionally check field, copy, notify.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
False positive — each copy handler is 2-3 lines with different guard conditions (plan_id vs pr_number) and different command format strings. Per AGENTS.md: "three similar lines of code is better than a premature abstraction." The handlers are intentionally kept inline for readability.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-23 04:50 PST_

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body: **Dignified Code Simplifier**: The `copy_close_plan`, `copy_fix_conflicts_remote`, and `copy_address_remote` blocks duplicate the same pattern. Extract as a helper method to eliminate duplication - all follow: format command, optionally check field, copy to clipboard, notify.

comments.author: schrockn
comments.body:
False positive — same reasoning as app.py: each handler has different guard logic and format strings. Keeping them inline follows the project principle of avoiding premature abstractions for 2-3 line patterns.

_Addressed via `/erk:pr-address` at 2026-02-23 04:50 PST_
